cran.flavors: r-devel-linux-x86_64-debian-clang, r-devel-linux-x86_64-debian-gcc, r-devel-linux-x86_64-fedora-clang, r-devel-linux-x86_64-fedora-gcc, r-devel-macos-arm64, r-devel-windows-x86_64, r-patched-linux-x86_64, r-release-linux-x86_64, r-release-macos-arm64, r-release-macos-x86_64, r-release-windows-x86_64, r-oldrel-macos-arm64, r-oldrel-macos-x86_64, r-oldrel-windows-x86_64
cran.message:
checkRd: (-1) ivmodel.Rd:37: Lost braces
       37 | and produces statistics for \eqn{\beta}. In particular, \code{ivmodel} computes the OLS, TSLS, k-class, limited information maximum likelihood (LIML), and Fuller-k  (Fuller 1977) estimates of \eqn{\beta} using \code{KClass}, \code{LIML}, and code{Fuller}. Also, \code{ivmodel} computes confidence intervals and hypothesis tests of the type \eqn{H_0: \beta = \beta_0} versus \eqn{H_0: \beta \neq \beta_0} for the said estimators as well as two weak-IV confidence intervals, Anderson and Rubin (Anderson and Rubin 1949) confidence interval (Anderson and Rubin 1949) and the conditional likelihood ratio confidence interval (Moreira 2003). Finally, the code also conducts a sensitivity analysis if \eqn{Z} is one-dimensional (i.e. there is only one instrument) using the method in Jiang et al. (2015).
          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       ^
   checkRd: (-1) ivmodelFormula.Rd:42: Lost braces
       42 | and produces statistics for \eqn{\beta}. In particular, \code{ivmodel} computes the OLS, TSLS, k-class, limited information maximum likelihood (LIML), and Fuller-k  (Fuller 1977) estimates of \eqn{\beta} using \code{KClass}, \code{LIML}, and code{Fuller}. Also, \code{ivmodel} computes confidence intervals and hypothesis tests of the type \eqn{H_0: \beta = \beta_0} versus \eqn{H_0: \beta \neq \beta_0} for the said estimators as well as two weak-IV confidence intervals, Anderson and Rubin (Anderson and Rubin 1949) confidence interval (Anderson and Rubin 1949) and the conditional likelihood ratio confidence interval (Moreira 2003). Finally, the code also conducts a sensitivity analysis if \eqn{Z} is one-dimensional (i.e. there is only one instrument) using the method in Jiang et al. (2015).
          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       ^

Maintainer: ‘Luke McGuinness <luke.mcguinness@bristol.ac.uk>’
   
   Package CITATION file contains call(s) to old-style citEntry().  Please
   use bibentry() instead.
   
   The BugReports field in DESCRIPTION has
     https://github.com/mcguinlu/robvis
   which should likely be
     https://github.com/mcguinlu/robvis/issues
   instead.

metrics.name: best_practices
metrics.description: Does repo have a best practices badge?

metrics.name: burstiness
metrics.description: A measure of the 'burstiness' in all development activity (code, issues, comments). Lower numbers reflect more consistent development activity.

metrics.name: change_req_n_closed
metrics.description: Number of recently closed change requests; log-transformed.

metrics.name: change_req_n_opened
metrics.description: Number of recently opened change requests; log-transformed.

metrics.name: change_req_prop_code
metrics.description: The proportion of code contributed through change requests.

metrics.name: change_req_prop_merged
metrics.description: Proportion of change requests which are merged

metrics.name: code_change_lines
metrics.description: Total number of code lines changed (added or removed); log-transformed.

metrics.name: commit_count
metrics.description: Total number of commits made to source code; log-transformed.

metrics.name: committer_count
metrics.description: Number of individuals who committed to source code

metrics.name: contrib_absence_commits
metrics.description: Number of contributors responsible for at least half of all contributions; log-transformed

metrics.name: cran_downloads
metrics.description: Number of downloads of packages on CRAN

metrics.name: ctb_count
metrics.description: Number of contributors of all kinds (to source code, through issues, pull-requests, comments); log-transformed

metrics.name: ctb_diversity
metrics.description: Simplified version of CHAOSS metrics, measured as total number of non-primary contributors; log-transformed

metrics.name: defect_resolution_dur
metrics.description: Time taken (in days) to resolve defects; log-transformed

metrics.name: dependency_count
metrics.description: Number of dependencies, or number of other packages this package depends on; log-transformed

metrics.name: elephant_factor
metrics.description: To what extent does package depend on single organization? Measure as number of affiliated organizations accounting for at least half of all contributions; log-transformed.

metrics.name: has_ci
metrics.description: Does package have continuous integration (true/false)?

metrics.name: issue_age
metrics.description: Average age of open issues, in days; log-transformed.

metrics.name: issue_cmt_count
metrics.description: Average number of comments per open issue; log-transformed

metrics.name: issue_response_time
metrics.description: Average time (in days) to respond to newly-opened issues; log-transformed.

metrics.name: issue_updates
metrics.description: Number of open issues updated in recent time period; log-transformed

metrics.name: issues_active
metrics.description: Number of actively opened or updated issues during recent time period; log-transformed

metrics.name: issues_closed
metrics.description: Number of issues closed in recent time period; log-transformed

metrics.name: issues_to_prs
metrics.description: Ratio of numbers of open issues to open pull requests

metrics.name: label_inclusivity
metrics.description: Proportion of recently opened or updated issues with inclusive labels (such as 'Good First Issue').

metrics.name: languages
metrics.description: Inverse mean proportion of code-lines-per-computer-language. Code distributed across more languages generates lower mean proportions, and so higher inverse values here. Higher values indicated more even distribution across more languages.

metrics.name: libyears
metrics.description: The CHAOSS 'libyears' metric, which is low or negative when dependencies are up-to-date, and becomes more positive for projects which have not updated their dependencies for longer times. Measured in days, and log-transformed

metrics.name: license_coverage
metrics.description: 'License coverage' metric, as proportion of source code files with explicitly declared licenses

metrics.name: licenses_declared
metrics.description: Does the package declare a recognised open-source license (true/false)?

metrics.name: maintainer_count
metrics.description: Total number of recently active maintainers, as contributors to source code, issues, or pull requests; log-transformed

metrics.name: num_commits
metrics.description: Number of code changes, or 'commits', during recent time period; log-transformed

metrics.name: num_contributors
metrics.description: Total number of recent contributors to source code

metrics.name: num_forks
metrics.description: Number of forks in recent time period; log-transformed

metrics.name: num_stars
metrics.description: Number of GitHub stars in recent time period; log-transformed

metrics.name: pr_age
metrics.description: Average age of pull requests in days; log-transformed

metrics.name: pr_closure_ratio
metrics.description: Proportion of all pull requests from recent time period which were closed.

metrics.name: pr_cmt_count
metrics.description: Average number of comments on recent pull requests (PRs); log-transformed

metrics.name: pr_response_durations
metrics.description: Average time taken (in days) for primary contributors to respond to new pull requests; log-transformed

metrics.name: pr_review_duration
metrics.description: Average time taken (in days) for pull request reviews; log-transformed

metrics.name: pr_reviews_approved
metrics.description: Number of all recent pull requests (PRs) which were approved; log-transformed

metrics.name: pr_revs_rejected
metrics.description: Number of all recent pull requests (PRs) which were rejected; log-transformed

metrics.name: recent_releases
metrics.description: Number of releases, either on GitHub or CRAN, in recent time period; log-transformed

metrics.name: release_freq
metrics.description: Average number of days between recent releases, either on GitHub or CRAN; log-transformed

metrics.name: response_time
metrics.description: Average time taken (in days) to respond to anything requiring attention (issues, pull request, CRAN errors); log-transformed

metrics.name: test_coverage
metrics.description: Proportion of all code covered by automated tests

metrics.name: time_to_close
metrics.description: Average time taken (in days) to close all recently-opened issues; log-transformed

metrics.name: watcher_count
metrics.description: Nr. repository 'watchers'; log-transformed

metrics.name: pr_duration
metrics.description: Average duration (in days) of pull requests; log-transformed
